name: United States
travel_notes: Domestic flights and rental cars are common for Cottonwood Canyon trips.
driving_notes: Winter canyon road restrictions and traction rules can affect rental-car feasibility.

provenance.display_caveat: Country/region summary is source-backed but not live travel advice.

name: Japan
travel_notes: International arrivals often connect through Tokyo before New Chitose for Hokkaido ski trips.
train_or_shuttle_notes: Niseko/Rusutsu trips can be done with bus/shuttle or train plus local transfer.
cash_card_notes: Carry some cash for rural transit, lockers, and smaller restaurants.

provenance.source_name: Japan National Tourism Organization travel reference
provenance.display_caveat: Country summary is source-backed but not live travel/entry advice.

name: Canada
travel_notes: Whistler and Interior BC require mountain-road and transfer planning from gateway airports.
driving_notes: Winter tires, mountain passes, and storm timing materially affect transfer reliability.

provenance.display_caveat: Country summary is source-backed but not live travel advice.

name: Austria
travel_notes: Arlberg/Tyrol trips commonly combine rail or shuttle transfers from Zurich or Innsbruck.
train_or_shuttle_notes: European rail and resort shuttles can reduce rental-car need but schedules must be checked for exact dates.

provenance.display_caveat: Country summary is source-backed but not live travel/entry advice.

name: Switzerland
travel_notes: Swiss Alps trips can use Zurich or Geneva gateways plus rail/shuttle transfers into resort valleys.
train_or_shuttle_notes: Rail is a strong no-car planning mode for Zermatt/Valais and other Swiss resort corridors.
driving_notes: Mountain-pass, tunnel, parking, and winter equipment rules should be checked before self-driving.

provenance.display_caveat: Source-backed planning record; verify current operations, access, and conditions before booking.

name: France
travel_notes: French Alps trips commonly use Geneva or Lyon gateways for Chamonix and Tarentaise resorts.
train_or_shuttle_notes: Rail, coach, and resort shuttles can be viable but schedules vary by valley and date.
driving_notes: Alpine winter equipment rules, tunnel timing, and parking logistics should be verified before self-driving.

provenance.display_caveat: Source-backed planning record; verify current operations, access, and conditions before booking.

name: Italy
travel_notes: Dolomites trips commonly use Venice, Milan, Verona, or Innsbruck gateways depending on resort valley.
train_or_shuttle_notes: Rail plus bus/shuttle works for some valleys but often needs schedule planning.
driving_notes: Dolomite resort hopping may favor a car; verify winter tires/chains and mountain road conditions.

provenance.display_caveat: Source-backed planning record; verify current operations, access, and conditions before booking.

name: Finland
travel_notes: Finnish Lapland trips often connect through Helsinki to Kittilä, Rovaniemi, or Kuusamo.
train_or_shuttle_notes: Domestic flights plus resort buses/taxis are common for Lapland ski trips.
driving_notes: Winter darkness, snowpack, and long distances make transfer planning important even on lower alpine terrain.

provenance.source_name: Visit Finland Lapland winter travel reference
provenance.display_caveat: Source-backed planning record; verify current operations, access, and conditions before booking.
